examples.title: Translation Matrix Generation
examples.description: Generate 3×3 translation matrix for given tx, ty
examples.explanation: Creates homogeneous transformation matrix for translating points by (tx, ty) offset.

examples.title: Rotation Matrix Generation
examples.description: Generate rotation matrix for counterclockwise rotation by angle θ
examples.code:
FUNCTION CreateRotationMatrix(theta)
 cos_theta = COS(theta)
 sin_theta = SIN(theta)
 RETURN [
 [cos_theta, -sin_theta, 0],
 [sin_theta, cos_theta, 0],
 [0, 0, 1]
 ]
END FUNCTION

// Convert degrees to radians
theta_rad = angle * PI / 180
M_rotate = CreateRotationMatrix(theta_rad)
examples.explanation: Creates rotation matrix using trigonometric functions. θ in radians for computation.

examples.title: Scaling Matrix Generation
examples.description: Generate scaling matrix with factors sx and sy
examples.code:
FUNCTION CreateScalingMatrix(sx, sy)
 RETURN [
 [sx, 0, 0],
 [0, sy, 0],
 [0, 0, 1]
 ]
END FUNCTION

// Uniform scaling
M_scale_uniform = CreateScalingMatrix(2, 2)
// Non-uniform scaling
M_scale_nonuniform = CreateScalingMatrix(2, 0.5)
examples.explanation: Creates scaling matrix. Uniform scaling uses equal sx and sy values.

examples.title: Matrix Multiplication for Composite Transformations
examples.description: Multiply transformation matrices to get composite transformation
examples.code:
FUNCTION MultiplyMatrices(A, B)
 // A and B are 3×3 matrices
 RESULT = [[0,0,0], [0,0,0], [0,0,0]]
 FOR i = 0 TO 2
 FOR j = 0 TO 2
 FOR k = 0 TO 2
 RESULT[i][j] = RESULT[i][j] + A[i][k] * B[k][j]
 END FOR
 END FOR
 END FOR
 RETURN RESULT
END FUNCTION

// Composite: Translate(2,3) then Rotate(45°)
M1 = CreateTranslationMatrix(2, 3)
M2 = CreateRotationMatrix(45)
M_composite = MultiplyMatrices(M1, M2)
// Apply to point (x,y): P' = M_composite × [x, y, 1]
examples.explanation: Matrix multiplication combines multiple transformations. Order: apply rightmost matrix first.

examples.title: Apply Transformation to Point
examples.description: Apply transformation matrix to a 2D point using homogeneous coordinates
examples.code:
FUNCTION ApplyTransformation(M, point)
 // M is 3×3 matrix, point is (x, y)
 x = point.x
 y = point.y
 
 // Convert to homogeneous: [x, y, 1]
 x_new = M[0][0]*x + M[0][1]*y + M[0][2]*1
 y_new = M[1][0]*x + M[1][1]*y + M[1][2]*1
 w = M[2][0]*x + M[2][1]*y + M[2][2]*1
 
 // Convert back from homogeneous
 IF w != 1 THEN
 x_new = x_new / w
 y_new = y_new / w
 END IF
 
 RETURN (x_new, y_new)
END FUNCTION

// Example: Rotate point (1,0) by 90°
M = CreateRotationMatrix(90)
result = ApplyTransformation(M, (1, 0))
// Returns approximately (0, 1)
examples.explanation: Transforms point by matrix multiplication. Handles homogeneous coordinate division for perspective cases.

examples.title: Rotate About Arbitrary Point
examples.description: Rotate a point about any pivot point using composite transformations
examples.explanation: Three-step process: translate pivot to origin, rotate, translate back. Uses matrix multiplication for efficiency.
